source_pr.title: planner: stabilize TestEnforceMPP predicate ordering
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #66939

Problem Summary:

`TestEnforceMPP` was flaky because TiFlash predicate-group ordering could change across runs. When two predicate groups had the same selectivity and size, their final order depended on Go map iteration order. The test used `require.Eventually` to hide that nondeterminism, so the failure still surfaced intermittently.

### What changed and how does it work?

- Replace `require.Eventually` in `TestEnforceMPP` with a direct `MustQuery().Check(...)` assertion.
- Make `groupByColumnsSortBySelectivity` deterministic by adding a stable tie-breaker on the expression-group code after selectivity and group-size comparisons.
- Update the recorded plan output to match the deterministic predicate placement.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
  - `go test -run TestEnforceMPP -tags=intest,deadlock -count=5 ./pkg/planner/core/casetest/enforcempp`
  - `make lint`
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
  * Replaced flaky asynchronous test waits with direct synchronous checks to make query plan tests more reliable.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved deterministic ordering in predicate handling so execution plans are consistent and reproducible.
  * Adjusted predicate pushdown behavior, which may change the order of predicates shown in explain plans.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

analysis:
PR title: planner: stabilize TestEnforceMPP predicate ordering
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/66940
Triage sources: manual_triage, explicit_flaky_signal, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: planner: stabilize TestEnforceMPP predicate ordering | `TestEnforceMPP` was flaky because TiFlash predicate-group ordering could change across runs. When two predicate groups had the same selectivity and size, their final order depended on Go map iteration order. The test used `require.Eventually` to hide that nondeterminism, so the failure still surfaced intermittently. | Subject: [PATCH 1/2] pkg/planner: stabilize TestEnforceMPP predicate ordering | require.Eventually(t,
Changed files (3): pkg/planner/core/casetest/enforcempp/enforce_mpp_test.go, pkg/planner/core/casetest/enforcempp/testdata/enforce_mpp_suite_out.json, pkg/planner/core/operator/physicalop/tiflash_predicate_push_down.go
Diff highlights (excerpt):
- require.Eventually(t,
- "      └─TableFullScan 10.00 mpp[tiflash] table:s pushed down filter:eq(test.s.a, 10), keep order:false, stats:pseudo"
- "      └─TableFullScan 10.00 mpp[tiflash] table:s pushed down filter:isnull(test.s.b), keep order:false, stats:pseudo"
- // Sort exprGroups by selectivity in ascending order
- // Keep the group order deterministic when selectivity and group size tie.
Full patch analyzed; see patch_url.
